sections.title: Prepositional Case: Location
sections.tip: The prepositional case always follows a preposition. With в/на it indicates location. Masculine/neuter nouns take -е, feminine nouns ending in -а take -е, feminine nouns ending in -ь take -и.
sections.explanation: The prepositional case (предложный падеж) always appears after prepositions: в (in), на (on/at). It answers 'where?' (где?), 'in what?' (в чём?), 'on what?' (на чём?). Masculine nouns take -е (most) or -у (some common nouns); feminine -а → -е; feminine -ь → -и; neuter -о → -е, -ие → -ии. Some masculine nouns take stressed -у: в лесу, на полу, в шкафу.

examples.en: The book is on the table.
We are talking on the phone.

exercises.explanation: Банк → банке in the prepositional. Most masculine nouns take -е.
Город → городе. Masculine nouns take -е in the prepositional.
Улица → улице. Feminine nouns ending in -а take -е in the prepositional.
After в (location), use the prepositional: Москва → Москве.
Парк → парке (prepositional). Most masculine nouns take -е.

exercises.prompt: Put the noun in the prepositional case:
exercises.explanation: Библиотека → библиотеке. Feminine nouns ending in -а take -е in the prepositional.

exercises.prompt: Match the nominative to the prepositional form:
exercises.explanation: Most nouns take -е: школа→школе, книга→книге, река→реке. Some masculine nouns take stressed -у: лес→лесу, пол→полу.
В университете — prepositional. Университет → университете (-е ending).

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear:
exercises.audio: Она сидит на скамейке в парке.
acceptedAnswers: Она сидит на скамейке в парке.
Она сидит на скамейке в парке
exercises.explanation: На скамейке (on the bench): скамейка → скамейке. В парке: парк → парке.
Дом→доме; дорога→дороге; озеро→озере; аптека→аптеке; шкаф→шкафу (stressed -у, one of the exceptions).

sections.title: Prepositional Case: Topics
sections.tip: With о/об the prepositional case indicates the topic of speech or thought: думать о чём-то (to think about something), говорить о ком-то (to speak about someone).
sections.explanation: The prepositional case with о/об means 'about': думать о работе (to think about work), говорить о друге (to talk about a friend). Use 'об' before vowels: об Анне, об этом. The same endings apply: masculine/neuter -е, feminine -а → -е, feminine -ь → -и.

exercises.explanation: О + prepositional: работа → работе.
Погода → погоде. О погоде — about the weather.
Путешествие → путешествии. Nouns ending in -ие take -ии in the prepositional.
Море → море in the prepositional (not морю). Neuter nouns ending in -е keep -е in the prepositional case.
'Об' is used before words starting with a vowel: об экзамене.

exercises.prompt: Put the noun in the prepositional with о/об:
exercises.explanation: Жизнь → жизни. Feminine nouns ending in -ь take -и in the prepositional.

exercises.prompt: Match the nominative to the prepositional form after о/об:
exercises.explanation: Любовь→любви (-ь→-и); здоровье→здоровье (-ье stays); друг→друге; Анна→Анне (об before vowel); музыка→музыке.

exercises.translation: She told me about her new job.
exercises.explanation: О новой работе — about the new job. Both adjective and noun are in the prepositional.

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear:
exercises.explanation: Вспоминать о + prepositional: школа → школе.

exercises.prompt: Все говорят об ___. (этот фильм – this film, prepositional)
exercises.explanation: Этот → этом, фильм → фильме. Об этом фильме — about this film.

sections.title: All Six Cases Overview
sections.tip: Russian has six cases: именительный (nominative), родительный (genitive), дательный (dative), винительный (accusative), творительный (instrumental), предложный (prepositional).
sections.explanation: Russian has six grammatical cases: 1) Именительный (Nominative) — subject; 2) Родительный (Genitive) — possession, absence; 3) Дательный (Dative) — indirect object; 4) Винительный (Accusative) — direct object; 5) Творительный (Instrumental) — means/tool; 6) Предложный (Prepositional) — location, topic. Practice identifying which case is needed in each context.

examples.en: The book is on the table.

exercises.prompt: Which case is 'маме' in the sentence 'Я дал цветы маме'?
exercises.explanation: Маме here is dative — 'gave flowers TO mom'. Мама → маме (dative = to whom?).

exercises.prompt: Identify the case: 'Книга лежит на столе.' — столе is in the ___ case.
exercises.explanation: На столе — prepositional case. На + location always triggers the prepositional.

exercises.prompt: In 'Он пишет ручкой', which case is 'ручкой'?
exercises.explanation: Ручкой is instrumental — writing WITH a pen. Ручка → ручкой.
Видеть (to see) takes the accusative: девушка → девушку, красивая → красивую.

exercises.prompt: Match each sentence fragment to the case used:
exercises.explanation: Брата=genitive (possession); ручкой=instrumental (tool); столе=prepositional (location); другу=dative (recipient); дом=accusative (direct object).
Нет requires the genitive: сестра → сестры.

exercises.prompt: ___ работает в школе. (учитель — subject of sentence)
exercises.explanation: The subject of the sentence is in the nominative case: учитель (no change).

exercises.translation: Mom, give the book to Andrey!
exercises.explanation: Мама (nominative, address), книгу (accusative — direct object), Андрею (dative — indirect object/recipient).

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear:
exercises.explanation: Мне (dative), об этой книге (prepositional). Multiple cases in one sentence!
Книга: nom. книга, gen. книги, dat. книге, acc. книгу, inst. книгой, prep. книге. Note: dative and prepositional forms are the same for feminine -а nouns.
